title: Pointer integrity: every reference a skill defers to must exist and carry the named section
action: Per D5. Author scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs — auto-discovered by run_verify because it matches test_*.mjs under the `scripts` DISCOVERY_ROOT (scripts/run_verify.mjs:40-45), so NO registration edit is needed anywhere. It scans every skills/**/*.md (sources only — skip the rendered projections under .claude/, .agents/, .claude-plugin/, .codex-plugin/, which are byte-copies and would multiply every finding) and extracts each pointer to a reference document: backtick-quoted paths ending in .md that name a references/ file, in either the bare `references/foo.md` form or the skill-qualified `bee-hive/references/foo.md` form. For each pointer assert the target file EXISTS, resolving a bare reference relative to the citing skill's own directory. Additionally, where a pointer names a section — the prose conventions in use are `references/foo.md`'s `## Bar` and the parenthetical `(`references/foo.md`, "Bar")` and `-> references/foo.md ("Bar")` forms — assert the target file actually contains a heading matching that name. Report every finding as file:line with the offending line quoted, in the style of okf_instructions_fence.mjs:211-259. Ship a --selftest mode (same convention as okf_instructions_fence.mjs and release_manifest.mjs) driving tmp fixture roots, and it MUST include NEGATIVE CONTROLS: a fixture whose pointer names a missing file and a fixture whose pointer names a missing section must each be DETECTED. A gate that has never been shown to fail is not known to work, and this repo has already shipped one scan whose scope was too narrow to catch what it existed to catch. The bare (non-selftest) run must be GREEN against the repo as it stands today; if it is not, the finding is real and gets fixed in this cell — record any such fix in the trace.

truths: scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs runs inside node scripts/run_verify.mjs with no registration edit.
The bare run is green against the current tree, or the pointers it found broken were fixed in this cell and named in the trace.
--selftest proves a missing target file is DETECTED, not merely that valid pointers pass.
--selftest proves a pointer to a missing section is DETECTED.
Rendered skill projections are excluded, so one broken source pointer yields one finding and not five.
Every finding names file:line and quotes the offending line.
prohibitions: Never ship this check without a negative control — an all-green gate that cannot fail is the exact defect this cell exists to prevent downstream.
Never scan the rendered projection roots.
Never widen the scan to make a red go away; a real broken pointer gets fixed.

verify: node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs --selftest && node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check

trace.outcome: Authored scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs: every backtick-quoted references/*.md pointer in skills/**/*.md must resolve, and where a pointer names a section, the target must carry that heading. Auto-discovered by run_verify's test_*.mjs glob (suite count 89->90, zero registration edits). Rendered projection roots excluded by path prefix both structurally and via a separately-tested isExcludedProjection guard, so one broken source pointer yields one finding rather than five. The check earned its keep on first run: 3 REAL broken pointers found and fixed, not tuned away — skills/bee-swarming/SKILL.md:22 and :127 cited a bare references/routing-and-contracts.md that resolved against bee-swarming's own dir (which has no such file), fixed to the skill-qualified form; skills/bee-hive/CREATION-LOG.md:5 cited references/go-mode-pipeline.md, renamed to go-mode.md at adaptation time, fixed by de-backticking the retired name so it reads as history rather than a live pointer. DEVIATION: fixing those pointers touched skills/**, which forced re-running render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs and a second release_manifest --write beyond the cell's declared files — without it test_plugin_distribution.mjs goes red on a sha256 mismatch. Recorded rather than hidden; it is the same derived-artifact chain P75 exists to mechanize.
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":"scripts/okf_instructions_fence.mjs and scripts/release_manifest.mjs (the two --selftest conventions this check follows); scripts/run_verify.mjs:40-45 (the discovery roots that make registration unnecessary); scripts/test_plugin_distribution.mjs (the suite that goes red when skills/** changes without a render+manifest refresh).","tests_added_or_changed":"New scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs with a 14-case --selftest over tmp fixture roots: both required negative controls, positive and negative coverage of all three real section-naming forms (possessive-heading, parenthetical, arrow), bare-vs-skill-qualified resolution scoping, projection exclusion by prefix rather than substring, the one-finding-not-five proof, and a row asserting bee's own tree is clean.","red_failure_evidence":"The two negative controls are the red evidence, and they are permanent rather than a one-off observation — they run on every verify. Verbatim from the orchestrator's own independent --selftest run: 'PASS  NEGATIVE CONTROL: a pointer to a MISSING FILE is DETECTED' and 'PASS  NEGATIVE CONTROL: a pointer naming a MISSING SECTION is DETECTED (the target file exists, the heading does not)'. Separately, the check was observed failing against the real tree on its first bare run, reporting the 3 genuine broken pointers listed in the outcome; after fixing them the bare run reports 96 pointers checked, 0 broken. So the check is proven to fail on synthetic breakage AND to have caught real pre-existing breakage before being made green.","verification_run":"Orchestrator re-ran independently, not the worker's word: node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs --selftest -> 14 passed, 0 failed; node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs -> 96 reference pointer(s) checked across skills/**/*.md, 0 broken; node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check -> 499 file(s) match stored manifest; node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS run_verify: 90 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=67836ms, every suite green."}
trace.verify_output: test_skill_pointers --selftest: 14 passed, 0 failed — including both NEGATIVE CONTROLS proven DETECTED (missing file; missing section where the target file exists but the heading does not). Bare run: 96 reference pointer(s) checked across skills/**/*.md, 0 broken. release_manifest --check: 499 file(s) match stored manifest. Full node scripts/run_verify.mjs: 90 suite(s), wall=67836ms, all green — suite count rose 89->90, confirming discovery picked the new file up with zero registration edits.
trace.verify_command: node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs --selftest && node scripts/test_skill_pointers.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check
